steps.title: Read the endpoints and calculate the segment length
steps.content:
From the graph, the line segment connects the points (−5, 4) and (4, 10). Using the distance formula:
$$\text{Distance} = \sqrt{(4 - (-5))^2 + (10 - 4)^2} = \sqrt{9^2 + 6^2} = \sqrt{81 + 36} = \sqrt{117}$$
Factor 117: $117 = 9 \cdot 13$, so $\sqrt{117} = 3\sqrt{13}$

The shown leg has length $3\sqrt{13}$ units.

steps.title: Use the area formula to find the other leg
steps.content:
For a right triangle, Area $= \frac{1}{2} \times \text{leg}_1 \times \text{leg}_2$. Substitute the known values:
$$36\sqrt{13} = \frac{1}{2} \times 3\sqrt{13} \times \text{leg}_2$$
Multiply both sides by 2:
$$72\sqrt{13} = 3\sqrt{13} \times \text{leg}_2$$
Divide both sides by $3\sqrt{13}$:
$$\text{leg}_2 = \frac{72\sqrt{13}}{3\sqrt{13}} = 24$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
